name: Folly hazptr
summary: Facebook's production C++ hazard pointers — thread-cached hazptr_recs, RAII hazptr_holder, hazptr_obj_base<T>::retire(), multi-domain support. Thread-local hprec cache with dynamic sizing & decay. Link-counting API for graph reclamation with no reader overhead. Cohorts and synchronous reclamation extensions queued for C++29 (P3135R0). In production at Meta since 2017; reference implementation behind C++26 std::hazard_pointer.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Facebook's **Folly `hazptr`** is the production C++ implementation of hazard pointers and the reference implementation behind C++26 `std::hazard_pointer`. The API surface:

- Thread-cached `hazptr_rec`s (per-thread cache of hazard records to avoid global pool contention)
- RAII `hazptr_holder` for scoped acquisition
- `hazptr_obj_base<T>::retire()` for retirement (CRTP base class so the retired-list link lives in the user object)
- Multi-domain support so independent subsystems can have independent reclamation domains

The thread-local `hprec` cache uses **dynamic sizing & decay** (recent commits) — caches grow under load and shed unused records over time, balancing per-thread footprint against allocation churn.

sections.title: Link-counting, cohorts, C++29 extensions
sections.content:
Beyond the standard HP API, Folly hazptr ships several advanced features:

- **Link-counting API** for graph reclamation with no reader overhead — useful for object graphs where reachability cannot be tracked by a single hazard pointer per access
- **Cohorts** — batched retirement groups with bulk reclamation, cutting per-retire scan amortised cost
- **Synchronous reclamation** for shutdown / drain scenarios

The cohort and synchronous-reclamation extensions are queued for **C++29 standardisation in P3135R0**, building on the C++26 baseline.

In production at Meta since 2017 across many internal services. The implementation has been the reference behind C++26 `std::hazard_pointer` (P2530R3), so the standardised API is essentially a portable subset of Folly's mature design.
